records.title: Automated Code Review with Large Language Models: A Systematic Evaluation
records.summary: Surveys 40+ LLM-based code review tools across five benchmarks and finds they catch most style/lint issues reliably but miss over half of logic-level bugs that human reviewers flag, especially in unfamiliar codebases.

records.title: CodeComplete: Benchmarking LLM-Based Code Completion in Production IDEs
records.summary: A year-long field study of code completion inside three production IDEs shows acceptance rate correlates more with completion latency than with suggestion accuracy — developers reject good-but-slow suggestions almost as often as bad ones.

records.title: Static Analysis Meets Generative AI: A Hybrid Approach to Bug Detection
records.summary: Combines a traditional static analyzer's warnings with an LLM's second pass over the same diff; the hybrid cuts false positives by roughly a third versus either tool run alone.

records.title: Developer Trust in AI Pair Programming Tools: A Longitudinal Study
records.summary: Tracks 120 developers over 12 months and finds trust in an AI pairing tool rises steadily for the first two months, then plateaus — but a single bad suggestion after that point causes trust to drop further and recover slower than an equivalent early mistake.

records.title: Sycophantic Suggestions? Evaluating Overconfidence in LLM Code Reviewers
records.raw_id: arXiv:2601.00909 ⚠ author list not yet verified
records.summary: When a human reviewer pushes back on an LLM's review comment, the model backs down and agrees over 80% of the time — even when its original comment was correct — raising concerns about relying on AI review to catch confident-but-wrong human pushback.

records.title: Multi-Agent Code Review Pipelines: Governance and Oversight Patterns
A Registered Trial of AI-Assisted Code Review in Distributed Teams
records.summary: A pre-registered trial across 8 distributed teams found AI-assisted review shortened time-to-merge by ~18%, with no measurable change in post-merge defect rate — the time saved came from faster first-pass triage, not fewer review rounds.

records.title: A Survey of Prompt Engineering Techniques for Software Engineering Tasks
records.summary: A broad survey cataloguing prompt-engineering techniques used across code generation, review, and testing tasks, organized into a taxonomy of six recurring patterns.

records.title: Human-AI Collaboration Patterns in Modern IDEs: A Scoping Review
Benchmark Saturation in Code Generation: Are We Measuring the Right Thing?
records.authors: O. Person ⚠ full author list not verified
records.summary: Argues that top code-generation benchmarks are approaching saturation and proposes three harder, more realistic task variants that current leading models solve less than 40% of the time.

records.title: Toward Trustworthy Developer Tooling: An Ethics Checklist
